title: FenceableGeometry
description: READ-ONLY value-object shape describing the geofence configuration that lives on ANY model using the HasGeofence trait. The trait adds the standard 7 columns via the `$table->addGeofenceColumns()` blueprint macro on the consumer's own migration. Models with non-standard geometry storage override the Geofenceable interface methods directly and bypass column reads entirely.
x-value_object_note: Hydrated by FenceableResolver from any Geofenceable model. Never persisted to a dedicated table. This schema documents the columns the HasGeofence trait EXPECTS on the consumer's table, added via the blueprint macro.

x-blueprint_macro.name: Blueprint::addGeofenceColumns()
x-blueprint_macro.target_table: consumer's own table (branches, facilities, venues, events, delivery_zones, home_addresses, ...)

geofence_polygon.description: The fence, drawn as a simple polygon (no holes on day 1).

geofence_radius_m.description: Radius in meters for radius-mode evaluation. Used only when geofence_polygon IS NULL.

geofence_accuracy_tolerance_m.description: Reject GPS readings noisier than this. Consumer sees SKIPPED.

geofence_enforcement_enabled.description: Consumer feature toggle — evaluator ignores this, consumers read it to decide whether to block on OUTSIDE.

geofence_updated_at.description: When the fence was last edited. Powers 'stale fence' surfacing.

index_added.predicate: geofence_polygon IS NOT NULL OR location_point IS NOT NULL

fenceableType.description: Snake-case alias of the fenceable model (from #[Geofenceable]).

tenantId.description: Owning tenant — pulled from the fenceable model via BelongsToTenant.

geofencePolygon.description: Magellan Polygon. Wire shape: { type: 'Polygon', coordinates: [[[lng, lat], ...]], srid: 4326 }.
